results.candidate: Station-4 grapple pit — BOTH robots fall in (safe pit, no death escape)

recoveries.name: U13: grapple zips UP to anchor (36,9), lands on rim, reels buddy out (DOWN+ACTION)
recoveries.note: grapple self-zipped out (ty<14=true); buddy reeled out (ty<14=true)

repro: equip grapple@x23, heavy@x26; gate opens
both walk right off the x33 rim into the 4-tile Station-4 pit (x34-38, floored by the world bottom, no hazard)
neither can die (no '^' floor) and neither can jump 4 tiles out
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — grapple self-zips out of the pit then reels the buddy out; no restart needed.
results.notes: The safe pit cannot kill (floored by the world bottom, GameScene 3279 only fires below worldH+60). The ONLY escape is the grapple zip-up + reel — role-symmetric because the grapple robot always carries the zip. Both assignments behave the same.

results.candidate: Station-4 grapple pit — heavy falls in, grapple free on the rim

recoveries.name: U13b: grapple on the rim reels the stranded buddy out (DOWN+ACTION)

repro: equip both; gate opens
heavy walks off the x33 rim into the pit; grapple stays on the rim
heavy cannot climb 4 tiles out alone
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — grapple on the rim reels the stranded buddy out.
results.notes: Asymmetric case (one in, one free). If the GRAPPLE robot is the one that falls in, the both-in scenario covers it (grapple self-zips). If the HEAVY falls in, the free grapple reels it out — this test.

results.candidate: Roller yard — solo Tiny walled by shimmer pillars without a Phase escort

recoveries.name: U5 Phase hand-hold escort through the shimmer pillars (buddy within 78px passes)

repro: full-lane traversal to the merge zone (x45-46)
Tiny alone tries to pass the shimmer pillar at x50 — it collides for non-phase robots and her jump is a hair short, so she's WALLED
recovery: Phase escorts hand-in-hand (within 78px the shimmer lets the buddy through)
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — solo Tiny is walled by the shimmer pillars (confirmed she can't bull through and can't die — rollers ignore Tiny), but the Phase escort walks her through. Phase is always free to return; if Phase is stuck it can die to a roller and respawn at the shared x46 checkpoint to reunite.
results.notes: The shimmer pillars are the U5 lesson: a solo non-phase robot is silently walled. Not a hard softlock while the partner is free to escort (or to die-and-reunite via the global checkpoint).

results.candidate: x14-19 electric pit — a robot falls in before learning the belt-gap zip

recoveries.name: hazard death → automatic respawn at the shared active checkpoint (safe ground)
recoveries.note: respawned alive on solid ground at tx=2.5 ty=13.5 (dead=false)

repro: equip grapple@x5, heavy@x8; gate opens
grapple zips the belt gap to anchor (17,9), then releases straight DOWN into the electric pit (x14-19, '^' floor at r16)
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — a hazard pit KILLS and respawns the robot at the shared checkpoint on safe ground; a 'both fall in' outcome is the same (both respawn). No restart needed.
results.notes: Contrast with the tutorial Station-4 pit: THAT one has no '^' floor so it cannot kill (the trap). This one is a hazard → death → shared-checkpoint respawn (GameScene 3275). Checkpoints are global, so both robots always respawn together on solid ground.

results.candidate: Co-op lift (needs both, threshold 3) death-loop + x50 landing-pit respawn-strand

stuck.note: team at the lift / x50 landing

recoveries.name: lift needs BOTH (weight 3) — a puzzle gate, not a lock; either robot can re-board and the lift returns home when unweighted
recoveries.note: lift rises only at weight 3 (heavy 2 + grapple 1)

recoveries.name: x50 landing-pit: U4 forward-step climb-out (auto-hop the x51 riser); lift re-ride fallback

repro: full 1-1: both weigh the lift (heavy=2 + grapple=1 = 3) to rise; grapple carries heavy across to the terrace
a short terrace jump drops into the x50-51 landing strip (solid, NOT hazard) — the U4 forward-step (x51 2-tile riser) climbs out; lift re-ride is the fallback
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — the lift is a needs-both puzzle gate (not a softlock); the x50 landing is solid floor with the U4 forward-step out. The U4 terrace checkpoint (x52,8) sits on solid ground, NOT over the open shaft, so no respawn-strand.
results.notes: Respawn-strand audit: 1-1 checkpoints are at x23, x40 and the terrace x52,8 — all on solid floor. The U4 note in level1_1.js explicitly moved the post-lift checkpoint OFF the lift runway (which could drop a respawn into the open shaft) onto the terrace. Lift returns home when unweighted (GameScene 3567) so it never traps a lone rider.

results.candidate: Heavy holds plate plA (opens sky-door b1) — who frees the holder?

stuck.note: heavy on the plate holding b1 for the buddy

recoveries.name: holder frees ITSELF: heavy steps off the momentary plate and walks on (crusher-immune)
recoveries.note: heavy walked off to tx=26.5 (b1 auto-closed=true)

repro: equip both; gate opens
heavy parks on plate plA (x15-16) → sky-door b1 opens; grapple takes the sky route over the crushers
heavy then simply STEPS OFF the plate and walks the tunnel — b1 is momentary/non-latch and heavy doesn't need it
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — no strand: b1 is a momentary (non-latch) core-route barrier; once the grapple buddy is across, the heavy just walks off the plate. The holder needs no external release.
results.notes: b1 (needs plates:[plA]) gates the optional sky route; the base traversal never seals heavy behind the plate.

results.candidate: Reel-across electric chasm — grapple crosses, heavy stranded on the near side

stuck.note: heavy on the near chasm edge; grapple across

recoveries.name: grapple anchor-reel pulls the stranded heavy across the chasm (DOWN+ACTION); zip-back available via the same anchors

repro: full 1-2 traversal: at the chasm (x40-52) grapple zips the anchor chain to the pillar/far floor and REELS heavy across (DOWN+ACTION)
the reel is the recovery for a stranded partner; anchors on both sides also allow a grapple zip-BACK retrace
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — the anchor-reel that completes the crossing IS the un-strand: a partner left on the near side is reeled over. Grapple can also zip BACK across the anchors to retrace.
results.notes: Anchors at 43/46/52 straddle the pillar so grapple can reel the buddy across OR zip back. And since checkpoints are global, a stranded heavy that steps into the electric chasm respawns at the shared checkpoint (x54 once grapple activates it) — death can also reunite the team across the gap.

results.candidate: HIGHEST RISK — cross-lane timed doors (6.5s): can a team be SEALED between two closed doors?

recoveries.name: re-pull the cross-lane lever after the 6.5s timer expires — it pops back out and RE-FIRES the window
complete the relay (Tiny→lvA1→tDoorB→Phase); a botched window is always retryable

repro: equip; Phase ambushes w1 and stages at lvB1 (x24, bottom lane); Tiny stages just short of tDoorA (x26, top lane)
PROBE A: Phase pulls lvB1 → tDoorA opens 6.5s; DON'T cross; wait out the timer → tDoorA closes AND lvB1 pops back out
PROBE A: Phase RE-PULLS lvB1 → tDoorA re-opens (the re-open lever re-fires and is reachable by the free partner)
PROBE C: complete the relay — Tiny through tDoorA to lvA1 (x32) → tDoorB (x34) opens → Phase through
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — NO hard seal is possible. The two 6.5s doors are in SEPARATE lanes (tDoorA top, tDoorB bottom), so no single robot is ever between both. Each door's re-open lever is in the PARTNER's lane and always reachable; on timer expiry the lever pops back out and re-firing it re-opens the door. A door never closes on a robot standing in it (no crush), and global checkpoints let a death reunite the team. Drive-confirmed: door timed out → lever re-fired → door re-opened → relay completed.
results.notes: Definitive answer to the flagged highest-risk item: a team CANNOT be permanently sealed between two closed timed doors. Dependency chain is linear and rooted at lvB1, which Phase can always reach in the bottom lane. levers re-fire after the 6.5s window (GameScene 3430-3438 pops the lever off on expiry; pullLever re-arms it).

results.candidate: Finale throw-across gap — Phase throws Tiny; a miss respawns Tiny near-side

stuck.note: Phase carrying Tiny at the finale gap

recoveries.name: re-throw: a missed toss respawns Tiny at the shared x47 checkpoint (near side); Phase picks up and throws again

repro: full 2-3: Phase ambushes w3, carries Tiny through panel x49, and THROWS her across the electric gap (x52-58)
a missed throw drops Tiny in the gap (hazard) → she respawns at the shared x47 checkpoint (near side) → Phase re-throws
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — the throw crosses the gap; a miss drops Tiny into the electric gap (hazard) which respawns her at the shared x47 checkpoint on the NEAR side, where Phase re-picks-up and re-throws. No permanent strand.
results.notes: The gap is a hazard (electric), so a fumbled throw is self-correcting: Tiny dies and both are back at x47 together. Phase is never left unable to re-throw.

results.candidate: Tower reel-up — heavy falls, grapple at top must re-descend; + is the crane unwinnable?

stuck.note: heavy at the tower base, grapple up a ledge

recoveries.name: crane fight completes (yank plates + stomp pods) — NOT unwinnable
recoveries.note: progressed to 'both reach the exit -> complete'

recoveries.name: tower reel-up: grapple zips back down and re-reels a fallen heavy (rung retry)

repro: full 1-3: defeat the crane (yank plates + stomp pods), then the tower — grapple zips up the anchors and REELS heavy up ledge by ledge
if heavy falls off a ledge it lands on the tower-base floor (no hazard); grapple zips back down and re-reels (the route's rung() retry)
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — the crane is beatable (hard, not a hard-lock) and the tower reel-up re-descends/re-reels any fallen buddy. A heavy that falls lands on the tower-base floor (no hazard) and gets reeled again.
results.notes: Tower-base floor is solid (r14) so a fallen heavy never dies and never leaves the reel's reach. The crane door is latch-open once defeated. No permanent strand.

results.candidate: Cross-lane separation; fan-ride required for Tiny (thermally non-deterministic)

recoveries.name: escort + fan-ride + valve (retrace to the escort wall reunites the lanes)
recoveries.note: route complete=true (last: P holds plate pl1, T pulls lvF -> exit; both finish)

repro: full 2-2: Phase escorts Tiny through the entry wall; the FAN lifts Tiny to the high deck; the valve shuts the steam wall for Phase's corridor
the fan-lift + timed steam jets are fps-sensitive (the known 2-2 thermal flake on this box)
results.verdict: RECOVERABLE — the fan-ride/escort completes; the lanes rejoin at the reunion yard and either robot can retrace to the escort wall. Global checkpoints (x8, x17, x40) reunite on any death.
results.notes: Explicitly flagged non-deterministic per the verification protocol. Classification defers to a cleaner run; the mechanics (escort back through the wall, shared checkpoints) provide recovery.
